samples.gvd:
module calendar
(
    input CLK,
    input RST,
    output reg [5:0] Hours,
    output reg [5:0] Mins,
    output reg [5:0] Secs
);

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        Secs <= 0;
    end
    else begin
        if (Secs == 59) begin
            Secs <= 0;
        end
        else begin
            Secs <= Secs + 1;
        end
    end
end

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        Mins <= 0;
    end
    else begin
        if (Secs == 59 && Mins == 59) begin
            Mins <= 0;
        end
        else if (Secs == 59) begin
            Mins <= Mins + 1;
        end
    end
end

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        Hours <= 0;
    end
    else begin
        if (Hours == 23 && Mins == 59 && Secs == 59) begin
            Hours <= 0;
        end
        else if (Secs == 59 && Mins == 59) begin
            Hours <= Hours + 1;
        end
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="calendar">
    <description>Implement a perpetual calendar with seconds, minutes, and hours.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK">Clock input.</input>
        <input id="RST">Active high reset signal.</input>
        <output id="Hours" width_description="[5:0]">Current hours.</output>
        <output id="Mins" width_description="[5:0]">Current minutes.</output>
        <output id="Secs" width_description="[5:0]">Current seconds.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="MAX_SECONDS">59</parameter>
        <parameter id="MAX_MINUTES">59</parameter>
        <parameter id="MAX_HOURS">23</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        This module implements a perpetual calendar by continuously updating the seconds, minutes, and hours based on the clock signal and reset signal. 
        The seconds value increments every clock cycle, and when it reaches 59, it resets to 0 and increments the minutes value. 
        When both the seconds and minutes values reach 59, the hours value increments by 1, and the hours value wraps around to 0 when it reaches 23.
    </implementation>
</module>
